ucs.title: [LLM] Cinnamon Tempest GPO-based ransomware deployment via SYSVOL payload staging
ucs.description: Hunts the actor's signature enterprise-wide ransomware push: dropping Babuk-derived payloads (Night Sky, Cheers, Pandora, Rook, LockFile, AtomSilo) into SYSVOL\Policies and abusing modified Group Policy Objects to execute the binary on every domain-joined host. This T1484.001 + T1080 + T1021.002 + T1543.003 chain is characteristic of DEV-0401/BRONZE STARLIGHT and rare among other ransomware crews who prefer PsExec/affiliate-style propagation.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstSeen max(_time) as lastSeen values(Filesystem.file_name) as files values(Filesystem.user) as writer dc(Filesystem.dest) as write_hosts from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_path="*\\SYSVOL\\*\\Policies\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\NETLOGON\\*") AND (Filesystem.file_name="*.exe" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.bat" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.ps1" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.vbs") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.user _time span=1h
| where writer!="SYSTEM" AND writer!="LOCAL SERVICE" AND NOT match(writer,".*\\$$")
| `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`
| join type=inner dest [
    | tstats summariesonly=true count as exec_hosts dc(Processes.dest) as fanout values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.process_name) as procs from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process="*\\SYSVOL\\*" OR Processes.process="*GPUpdate*" OR Processes.parent_process_name="gpscript.exe" OR Processes.parent_process_name="taskeng.exe") by Processes.dest _time span=1h
    | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
    | where fanout>=5
  ]
| where lastSeen-firstSeen < 7200
| eval risk_score=fanout*10
| sort - risk_score
| table firstSeen dest writer files procs cmdlines fanout risk_score
ucs.kql:
let SysvolWrites = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FolderPath has_any (@"\SYSVOL\", @"\NETLOGON\", @"\Policies\{")
| where FileName endswith ".exe" or FileName endswith ".dll" or FileName endswith ".bat" or FileName endswith ".ps1" or FileName endswith ".vbs" or FileName endswith ".cmd"
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$" and InitiatingProcessAccountName !in~ ("system","local service","network service")
| project WriteTime=Timestamp, DC=DeviceName, Writer=InitiatingProcessAccountName, DroppedFile=FileName, DroppedPath=FolderPath, SHA256;
let GpoExec = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where (FolderPath has @"\SYSVOL\" or ProcessCommandLine has_any (@"\SYSVOL\", "gpupdate /force", "Invoke-GPUpdate"))
   or (InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("gpscript.exe","taskeng.exe","svchost.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has_any (@"\Policies\{", "-File ", ".dll,#"))
| summarize ExecHosts=dcount(DeviceName), FirstExec=min(Timestamp), Cmdlines=make_set(ProcessCommandLine,25) by FileName, SHA256
| where ExecHosts >= 5;
SysvolWrites
| join kind=inner GpoExec on SHA256
| extend MinutesToFanout = datetime_diff('minute', FirstExec, WriteTime)
| where MinutesToFanout between (-30 .. 240)
| project WriteTime, DC, Writer, DroppedFile, DroppedPath, SHA256, FirstExec, ExecHosts, Cmdlines
| order by ExecHosts desc
ucs.rationale: Microsoft and Secureworks both attribute SYSVOL/GPO-driven ransomware fan-out as a defining DEV-0401/BRONZE STARLIGHT TTP — they explicitly use Group Policy to encrypt entire estates rather than running PsExec from a beachhead like Conti/LockBit affiliates. Combining a non-machine-account write of an executable into SYSVOL\Policies with >=5-host fan-out within a couple of hours is something almost

ucs.title: [LLM] Cinnamon Tempest DLL side-loading of Cobalt Strike via signed Chinese-vendor binaries
ucs.description: Hunts the loader pattern Microsoft attributes to Cinnamon Tempest where signed third-party executables (e.g. BDReinit.exe, ZAM.exe, vmtoolsd-style binaries, IObit/Tencent/360 utilities) are dropped into non-standard paths and side-load a malicious DLL that decrypts a Cobalt Strike or PlugX-style payload — overlapping with toolkits also used by APT41/Bronze Atlas, which is why this group sits inside the 'BRONZE' ecosystem.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstSeen values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.parent_process) as parents from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("BDReinit.exe","ZAM.exe","vmtoolsd.exe","GUP.exe","AvastBrowserUpdate.exe","360se.exe","QQPCRTP.exe","IntelTrace.exe","hpqhvind.exe","GoogleUpdate.exe","OneDrive.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.user
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| where NOT (process_path LIKE "%\\Program Files%" OR process_path LIKE "%\\Program Files (x86)%" OR process_path LIKE "%\\Windows\\System32%")
| eval suspect_path=if(match(process_path,"(?i)\\\\(ProgramData|Users\\\\Public|AppData\\\\Roaming|Temp|Intel|PerfLogs)\\\\"),1,0)
| where suspect_path=1
| join type=inner dest process_name [
    | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.dat" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.log") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.process_name
    | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`
    | rename process_name as dropper
    | eval colocated=if(like(file_path,replace(dropper,"\.exe$","")+"%"),1,0)
  ]
| join type=inner dest [
    | tstats summariesonly=true count as net_cnt from datamodel=Network_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_port IN (80,443,8080,8443) AND NOT (All_Traffic.dest_category="corporate" OR All_Traffic.dest="10.0.0.0/8" OR All_Traffic.dest="192.168.0.0/16") by All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.dest_ip
    | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)`
  ]
| sort - firstSeen
| table firstSeen dest user process_name process_path cmdlines parents net_cnt
ucs.kql:
let SuspectLoaders = dynamic(["BDReinit.exe","ZAM.exe","vmtoolsd.exe","GUP.exe","AvastBrowserUpdate.exe","360se.exe","QQPCRTP.exe","IntelTrace.exe","hpqhvind.exe","GoogleUpdate.exe","OneDrive.exe","Notepad++.exe","vssadmin.exe"]);
let TrustedRoots = dynamic([@"c:\program files\", @"c:\program files (x86)\", @"c:\windows\system32\", @"c:\windows\syswow64\"]);
let Loader = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where FileName in~ (SuspectLoaders)
| extend FolderLow = tolower(FolderPath)
| where not(FolderLow has_any (TrustedRoots))
| where FolderLow has_any (@"\programdata\", @"\users\public\", @"\appdata\roaming\", @"\appdata\local\temp\", @"\perflogs\", @"\intel\", @"\windows\tasks\")
| project LoaderTime=Timestamp, DeviceName, DeviceId, Loader=FileName, LoaderPath=FolderPath, LoaderSHA=SHA256, LoaderPID=ProcessId, ParentName=InitiatingProcessFileName, ParentCmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
let ColocatedDll = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where FileName endswith ".dll" or FileName endswith ".dat" or FileName endswith ".log" or FileName endswith ".bin"
| where not(InitiatingProcessSignatureStatus == "Valid" and InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (TrustedRoots))
| project DropTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DllName=FileName, DllPath=FolderPath, DllSHA=SHA256, Dropper=InitiatingProcessFileName;
Loader
| join kind=inner ColocatedDll on DeviceId
| where tolower(DllPath) startswith tolower(LoaderPath) or tolower(substring(DllPath,0,strlen(DllPath)-strlen(DllName))) == tolower(LoaderPath)
| where abs(datetime_diff('minute', LoaderTime, DropTime)) <= 30
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where RemotePort in (80, 443, 8080, 8443)
    | where not(ipv4_is_private(RemoteIP))
    | summarize C2Count=count(), C2Hosts=make_set(RemoteUrl,10), C2IPs=make_set(RemoteIP,10) by DeviceId, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessId
) on $left.DeviceId == $right.DeviceId, $left.Loader == $right.InitiatingProcessFileName, $left.LoaderPID == $right.InitiatingProcessId
| project LoaderTime, DeviceName, Loader, LoaderPath, LoaderSHA, ParentName, ParentCmd, DllName, DllSHA, C2Count, C2Hosts, C2IPs
| order by LoaderTime desc
ucs.rationale: Cinnamon Tempest is repeatedly observed by Microsoft Threat Intelligence using DLL search-order hijacking against benign signed binaries — many of them Chinese-vendor utilities — to load Cobalt Strike beacons before deploying Babuk-derived ransomware. Anchoring on (a) a curated list of binaries publicly tied to BRONZE STARLIGHT/DEV-0401 loader chains, (b) execution from non-trusted directories lik
